steps.title: Recognize the discriminant condition
steps.content: A quadratic equation has exactly one solution when its discriminant equals zero. In our equation $-2x^2 + 20x + c = 0$, identify the coefficients: $a = -2$, $b = 20$, and the unknown $c$.

steps.title: Solve for c using the discriminant condition
steps.content:
Set the discriminant equal to zero and solve for $c$:
$$20^2 - 4(-2)c = 0$$
$$400 + 8c = 0$$
$$c = -50$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
